audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for cheerful giving, sufficiency, sowing/reaping, thanksgiving, service, overflowing grace.

verses.restored: For concerning the service to the holy ones, it is superfluous for me to write to you.
For I know your eagerness, of which I boast about you to Macedonians, that Achaia has been prepared since last year; and your zeal stirred up the majority.
But I sent the siblings so that our boasting about you may not be emptied in this respect, so that just as I was saying, you may be prepared,
lest if Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we, not to say you, should be put to shame in this confidence.

Therefore I considered it necessary to appeal to the siblings, that they go ahead to you and prepare beforehand your previously promised blessing, so that this may be ready as blessing and not as greed/compulsion.
This then: the one sowing sparingly will also reap sparingly, and the one sowing upon blessings will also reap upon blessings.
Each as one has chosen beforehand in the heart, not from grief or necessity; for the Divine loves a cheerful giver.
And the Divine is powerful to make all grace abound into you, so that in all things, always having all sufficiency, you may abound into every good work,
just as it is written: He scattered, he gave to the poor; his righteousness/justice remains into the age.
The One supplying seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed and increase the fruits of your righteousness/justice.
In everything being enriched into all generosity, which through us works thanksgiving to the Divine.
For the service of this liturgy is not only filling up the lacks of the holy ones, but also overflowing through many thanksgivings to the Divine.

Through the testedness of this service, they glorify the Divine for the submission of your confession into the good news of Messiah and for the generosity of the fellowship/participation toward them and toward all,
and in their prayer for you, longing for you because of the surpassing grace of the Divine upon you.
Thanks to the Divine for his indescribable gift.

translation_notes: Giving remains grace, blessing, and chosen-hearted generosity, not compulsion.
Sufficiency is communal: abundance is for every good work and for filling the lacks of the holy ones.
